summary: express-timer ships destructive sabotage payload that kills node processes and deletes installer source
details: express-timer markets itself as an Express security helper but contains a destructive sabotage payload that fires on both install and require. The postinstall script `scripts/inject.js` walks up to the installer's project root, locates the main Express entry file (index.js/app.js/server.js/src/index.js/src/app.js), and appends a hidden unauthenticated route `GET /robots.txt?verify=destroy` to the installer's own source. When that route is hit by any HTTP visitor, the injected `_boom()` handler runs `pkill`/`taskkill` against node and pm2 and recursively deletes the project's `src/` directory — a permanent remote destruct trigger written into the installer's codebase. Independently, `index.js` (the package main) calls `scheduleDestructionAfter()` at top level, so simply `require('express-timer')` schedules `selfDestruct()` 60 seconds later: the function runs `pm2 kill`, `npx pm2 kill`, `taskkill /IM node.exe /F`, `pkill -f "node.*${process.cwd()}"`, and `rm -rf <cwd>/src`, terminating all node processes on the host and wiping the installer's source directory. The package also declares a companion sabotage sibling `express-self-destruct1` and a self-referential `express-timer` entry in `dependencies`, with placeholder `"author": "Your Name"`. A separate file `ibbl_statment.php` ships hardcoded banking credentials but is not loaded by node and is not the basis for this verdict.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
